mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 across all historical rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and contraction in housing starts indicates consumer substitution away from durable goods, signaling weakening aggregate demand that produces negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS-HOUST divergence yields Rank IC < 0.01 over 60-day horizons, or forward returns are indistinguishable from random walk.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion during periods of elevated consumer price index levels triggers inflation fears and anticipated monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 70% of observations when INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL exceeds 310.0, invalidating the inflation-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index above 0.3 percent forces markets to price in delayed rate cuts, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) after 2022.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative or zero correlation with payroll growth, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates structural demand weakness that retail data captures faster, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth crosses below GDPC1 YoY growth
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in > 65% of divergence episodes, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production during a low federal funds rate environment signals an economic slowdown that prompts expectations of aggressive central bank intervention, driving positive equity returns over one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of low-rate industrial production declines, or the policy-easing expectation channel shows no statistical significance.
